steps.title: Apply the inverse hypothesis
steps.content: The hypothesis says birds with a narrower field of vision while foraging should be more sensitive to neighboring species' alarm calls. M. barbatus forages in dense vegetation, so it should react more; P. saturatus forages in open areas, so it should react less.

steps.title: Test the predicted contrast
steps.content: Choice A has M. barbatus not reacting, contrary to the prediction. Choice B does not compare the two focal species. Choice C shifts to M. barbatus alarm calls and has P. saturatus reacting. The supporting finding must show P. saturatus not reacting while M. barbatus does. That pattern clearly tests both sides of the inverse relationship at once. The answer is D.
